commands.review_summary:
The PR proposes changing Telegram media skip handling so existing ingest-enabled group or topic config can override unaddressed group media skipping, plus a regression test.

PR surface: Source +18, Tests +38. Total +56 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: unclear. the linked issue and PR body describe a plausible Telegram ingest/media path, but this review did not complete the source-level or live reproduction checks needed for high confidence.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is human PR review plus real Telegram proof, not an automated code repair from the incomplete evidence available here.

target.title: fix(telegram): honor ingest config before skipping unaddressed group media
target.body:
### Summary

When a Telegram group/topic has `ingest: true` configured, plugins should receive all messages (including unaddressed media-bearing ones) via `message:received` hooks. But `shouldSkipMediaDownloadForUnaddressedMentionGroup` returns `true` (skip) for unaddressed media BEFORE the ingest config is consulted, so plugins never see media messages in ingest-enabled groups — they only get text messages.

The text-only message path already respects `ingest` correctly in `bot-message-context.body.ts` (lines 438-441), checking `topicConfig?.ingest ?? groupConfig?.ingest ?? defaultConfig?.ingest`. The media path now replicates this same resolution hierarchy.

### What changed

- **`extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts`** — Before `mentionDecision.shouldSkip` returns `true`, resolve the ingest config via `resolveChannelGroupPolicy` (same pattern as text-message path). If `ingestEnabled === true`, return `false` so media proceeds to download and the `message:received` hook can fire.
- **`extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.channel-post-media.test.ts`** — Regression test: unaddressed media in a `requireMention + ingest: true` group is NOT skipped (`saveRemoteMedia` and `getFile` are called).

### What did NOT change (scope boundary)

- The `requireMention` skip logic itself — unchanged. Only the ingest override is new.
- The text-message ingest path — already correct, unchanged.
- The media download logic — unchanged. Only the skip gate is affected.
- Default behavior: groups without `ingest: true` still skip unaddressed media as before.

### Linked context

Closes #92067

### Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Telegram groups with `ingest: true` now receive unaddressed media-bearing messages via `message:received` hooks, instead of silently skipping them before download.
- **Environment tested**: Node v24.13.1 on Linux x86_64; vitest test exercising the actual bot handler production code path.
- **Steps run after the patch**: `node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.channel-post-media.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal capture of test output showing all 12 tests pass including the new ingest override test:

  ```
  ✓ createTelegramBot channel_post media > skips unmentioned requireMention group media before downloading (#81181)
  ✓ createTelegramBot channel_post media > does not skip unaddressed group media when ingest is enabled (#92067)
  ✓ createTelegramBot channel_post media > notifies mentioned requireMention groups when media download fails

  Tests  12 passed (12)
  ```

- **Observed result after the fix**: The new test passes. When `ingest: true` is set on a `requireMention` group, `saveRemoteMedia` and `getFile` are called (media is not skipped). When `ingest` is absent/false, the existing skip behavior (#81181) is preserved.
- **What was not tested**: Full end-to-end bot runtime with a live Telegram API connection and a real `message:received` hook invocation. Only the skip/download gate was tested via the production bot handler. The ingest hook invocation downstream was verified by matching the resolution hierarchy to the working text-message path (Proof 4: identical `topicConfig?.ingest ?? groupConfig?.ingest ?? defaultConfig?.ingest` pattern).
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints**: CMake 3.18 on this machine (< 3.19 required by node-llama-cpp), so `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` fails at node-llama-cpp postinstall. Core dependencies (vitest, tsx) are installed and functional. No live Telegram bot API for end-to-end testing.

### Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? **Yes** — groups with `ingest: true` now receive media-bearing messages via hooks instead of skipping them. Groups without `ingest` are unchanged.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? **No** — uses existing `ingest` config field, no new config needed.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? **No**
- Highest-risk area: The `resolveChannelGroupPolicy` call adds a small overhead per unaddressed media message. Mitigation: only called when `mentionDecision.shouldSkip` is true (the existing skip path), not for every message.
- Risk mitigation: Minimal additive change — only adds a config check before an existing `return true`. Default behavior (no ingest) is completely unchanged. Resolution hierarchy matches the established text-message pattern.
target.author: zoowh
